questions.question:
Fourier series of the periodic function (period \(2 \pi \)) defined by
\(f(x) = \begin{cases} 0, -p \lt x \lt 0\\x, 0 \lt x \lt p \end{cases} \text { is }\\ \frac{\pi}{4} + \Sigma \left [ \frac{1}{\pi n^2} \left(\cos n\pi - 1 \right) \cos nx - \frac{1}{n} \cos n\pi \sin nx \right ]\)
But putting \(x = \pi\), we get the sum of the series
\(1 + \frac{1}{3^2} + \frac{1}{5^2} + \frac{1}{7^2} + \cdots \text { is }\)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Refer to the PASCAL program shown below.

 Program PARAM (input, output);
var m, n : integer;
procedure P (var, x, y : integer);
var m : integer;
begin
m : = 1;
x : = y + 1
end;
procedure Q (x:integer; vary : integer);
begin
x:=y+1;
end;
begin
m:=0; P(m,m); write (m);
n:=0; Q(n*1,n); write (n)
end

The value of m, output by the program PARAM is:
options:
A.

1, because m is a local variable in P

B.

0, because m is the actual parameter that corresponds to the formal parameter in p

C.

0, because both x and y are just reference to m, and y has the value 0

D.

1, because both x and y are just references to m which gets modified in procedure P
questions.correct_answer:
B.

0, because m is the actual parameter that corresponds to the formal parameter in p
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Refer to the PASCAL program shown below.

Program PARAM (input, output);
var m, n : integer;
procedure P (var, x, y : integer);
var m : integer;
begin
m : = 1;
x : = y + 1
end;
procedure Q (x:integer; vary : integer);
begin
x:=y+1;
end;
begin
m:=0; P(m,m); write (m);
n:=0; Q(n*1,n); write (n)
end

The value of n, output by the program PARAM is:
options:
A.

0, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to x in procedure Q.

B.

0, because n is the actual parameter to y in procedure Q.

C.

1, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to x in procedure Q.

D.

1, because n is the actual parameter corresponding to y in procedure Q.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
For the program segment given below, which of the following are true?

 program main (output);
type link = ^data;
data = record
d : real;
n : link
end;
var ptr : link;
begin
new (ptr);
ptr:=nil;
.ptr^.d:=5.2;
write ln(ptr)
end.
options:
A.

The program leads to compile time error

B.

The program leads to run time error

D.

The program produces error relating to nil pointer dereferencing
questions.correct_answer:
D.

The program produces error relating to nil pointer dereferencing
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Assume that the following jobs are to be executed on a single processor system
\(\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline \textbf{Job Id} & \textbf{CPU Burst Time} \\\hline \text{p} & 4 \\\hline \text{q} & 1 \\\hline \text{r} & 8 \\\hline \text{s} & 1 \\\hline \text{t} & 2 \\\hline \end{array}\)
The jobs are assumed to have arrived at time \(0^+\) and in the order p,q,r,s,t. Calculate the departure time (completion time) for job p if scheduling is round robin with time slice 1

questions.question: Let S be an infinite set and \(S_1 \dots , S_n\) be sets such that \(S_1 \cup S_2 \cup \dots \cup S_n = S\). Then
options:
A.

at least one of the set \(S_i\) is a finite set

B.

not more than one of the set \(S_i\) can be finite

C.

at least one of the sets \(S_i\) is an infinite

D.

not more than one of the sets \(S_i\) can be infinite
questions.correct_answer:
C.

at least one of the sets \(S_i\) is an infinite
